compositional_truth_check.details: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P4 (Silver Guest) is present but in a state of severe decline.

plant_audit.P1: No visible plant matter. Soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: No visible plant matter. Soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remains, showing signs of chlorosis and edge necrosis. Rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.P4: Stem is largely desiccated; leaf tissue is translucent and collapsing.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently damp/dark; no signs of recent aeration.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4 pot; electronic component (header pin) present on desk surface near P4.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-3: Minor shift in desk debris; P4 leaf shows slight postural drooping.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: P4 leaf continues to lose turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Significant light reduction; P4 stem appears to have collapsed further into the soil.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Recovery of light levels reveals near-total necrosis of P4 specimen.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical/Terminal. The single leaf is failing to maintain structural integrity.
visual_health_inference.P4: Terminal. The specimen has lost all photosynthetic viability.

anomalies: Presence of electronic header pins on desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing in P4.
Total absence of P1 and P2 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of advanced collapse. P1 and P2 have been completely lost, leaving only bare soil. P3 is reduced to a single, struggling leaf. P4 is undergoing rapid senescence, likely due to root rot or severe environmental stress, evidenced by the translucent, necrotic tissue. The presence of non-botanical debris (eggshells, electronics) suggests an experimental or neglected environment.
